response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and web developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js experience and consistent software craftsmanship practices, but he falls critically short of the role's core minimum qualifications. The most disqualifying gap is C++: the position requires 8 years of C++ experience, while the resume shows at most 6–8 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project — C++ is not even listed in his skills section. He also lacks the required depth in large-scale infrastructure and distributed systems, and has no demonstrated cloud compute platform leadership or technical lead experience at meaningful scale.
strengths: Consistent application of software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles over a decade
Exposure to high-throughput distributed microservices at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second using low-latency async I/O techniques
Open-source platform ownership (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) demonstrates ability to drive technical projects end-to-end independently
concerns: Essentially no C++ experience — the role mandates 8 years, but the resume shows only a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis; C++ is absent from the skills section entirely
No cloud compute platform or systems infrastructure leadership; cloud exposure is limited to using AWS CDK as a deployment tool at Optilyz, not designing or owning platform-level architecture

No evidence of technical leadership at organizational scale — mentoring activity is informal (Dojos, workshops) and there is no record of leading platform teams, managing cross-team alignment, or owning large ambiguous infrastructure programs

key_factors.factor: Strong software craftsmanship and testing discipline
